step_by_step_thinking:
Symphyseal fractures typically occur due to high-energy trauma and may be unstable. Lag screws are frequently utilized to stabilize these fractures through load-sharing mechanisms rather than purely compressing the fractured segments. Therefore, considering the nature of symphyseal fractures and the function of lag screws:
\boxed{\text{B}}
To justify my selection:
- Symphyseal fractures benefit significantly from load-sharing devices like lag screws.
- Load-bearing would imply complete weight transmission through the fixated area, whereas load-sharing distributes stress among different parts of the construct, enhancing stability and healing potential. Given the context of symphyseal fractures needing robust support during healing, B (Load Sharing) best describes the mechanism involved here. Thus, I selected

- Load-bearing would imply complete weight transmission through the fixated area, whereas load-sharing distributes stress among different parts of the construct, enhancing stability and healing potential.
